sections.paragraph:
Ὀνήσιμος, ου, ὁ Onesimus, lit. ‘useful’ (s. the play on words in Phlm 11), a name freq. found (ins, pap: Mel. HE 4, 26, 13; reff. in Thieme 40; Preisigke, Namenbuch 1922 and in the lit. below), esp. for slaves (Menand., Epitr. 1 [p. 975] al.; Galen, De Optima Doctr. 1 p. 82 JMarquardt [1884] Ὀν. ὁ Πλουτάρχου δοῦλος; Lghtf., St. Paul’s Ep. to the Col. and to Phlm. 308f; Hatch 146; Zahn, Einleitung I3 325).

① A slave of Philemon, in whose interest Paul wrote Phlm. Phlm 10; subscr.; πιστὸς καὶ ἀγαπητὸς ἀδελφός Col 4:9. Cp. Col subscr.—ARobertson, Exp. 8th ser., 19, 1920, 29ff; EGoodenough, HTR 22, 1929, 181–83; PHarrison, ATR 32, ’50, 268–93; BHHW II 1343; JNordling, JSNT 41, ’91, 97–119 a runaway slave.

② An Onesimus appears as bishop of Ephesus in IEph 1:3; 2:1; 6:2; CIG 2983 attests the name for this city (cp. EGoodsp., Introd. to the NT ’37, 121f and ref. there to JKnox).—DELG s.v. ὀνίνημι. LGPN I. M-M.

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

freq. freq. = frequent(ly)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

Thieme Thieme = GT., Die Inschriften von Magnesia am Mäander und das NT—List 3

Preisigke, Namenbuch Preisigke, Namenbuch = Namenbuch 1922

esp. esp. = especially

Menand Menand , various dramas cited; IV–III b.c.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Galen Galen , II a.d.—List 5

Lghtf. Lghtf. = JBLightfoot

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

Hatch Hatch = WH.—List 3

Zahn Zahn = TZ., various works—List 6

subscr. subscr. = subscription

Exp. Exp. = Expositor—List 6

ser. ser. = series

HTR HTR = Harvard Theological Review—List 6

ATR ATR = Anglican Theological Review—List 6

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

JSNT JSNT = Journal for the Study of the NT—List 6

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

CIG CIG = Corpus Inscriptionum Graecarum—List 3

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

NT NT = New Testament

ref. ref. = reference(s)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

LGPN LGPN = A Lexicon of Greek Personal Names—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 711
